FT_IC1.evidence: "Cross-sectional study in children of 9-11 years old (n=2,952) and adolescents of 17-18 years old (n=261), from the Dutch ABCD Study and the Spanish INMA Project." Uses Dutch ABCD and Spanish INMA, not U.S. ABCD Study.

notes: Excluded under FT-EC1 (not U.S. ABCD Study)

FT_IC1.evidence: "survey and interview data collected for 9- to 11-year-olds from the 21-site adolescent brain cognitive development study" - confirms U.S. ABCD.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using generalized linear mixed models examining relationships between electronic media use and anxiety/depression measured by CBCL.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Child Psychiatry & Human Development (2019).

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 identifies qualifying phone/SM-specific estimates paired with individual-trait outcomes. Methods state analysis of "video gaming and video chatting" paired with child anxiety and depression (CBCL). Table 2 reports coefficients for child-reported weekday and weekend EMU paired with anxiety and depression outcomes (e.g., child reported weekday use with anxiety: β=0.055, p=.001).

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported throughout: Table 2 contains multiple coefficients, t-values, significance levels, and 95% CIs for EMU-anxiety and EMU-depression relationships.

FT_IC4.evidence: Generalized linear mixed models with reported inferential statistics (coefficients, t-values, p-values, CIs) for SM/phone variables paired with individual-trait outcomes.

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional, not longitudinal; EC7 does not apply. Cross-sectional carve-out confirmed.
notes: INCLUDE - meets all FT-IC criteria; EC7 does not apply to cross-sectional analysis.

FT_IC1.evidence: "Parents and children provided cross-sectional baseline data (2016-18) as part of the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development study, a broadly US representative sample of 11,875 children aged 9 to 10 years."

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using negative binomial structural equation modeling to examine mediation by sleep duration.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in International Journal of Behavioral Nutrition and Physical Activity (2019).

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 finds no phone/SM-specific estimate isolated from other screen types. Step 2 composite-content check fails: headline analysis uses composite "screen time" defined as "ST types (television/movies, videos, video games, and social media)" per methods. Composite bundles television/movies (non-phone/non-SM activity, §2.4 binding rule) with phone/SM items without separate breakout in main tables. Per criteria: "if the composite includes any non-phone/non-SM ingredient (TV...) without separate breakout → NOT_MET."

notes: EXCLUDE under FT-EC2 - composite includes TV without separate breakout; fails mechanical Step 2 of FT-IC2 two-step test.
